# WS2 verifiability report (multimodal): which of the 16 representations carry real content-signal

*Generated by `gate_multimodal.py`, 2026-06-13. Generalizes the ADMET-only gate (`verifiability_report.md`, 7/7 SMILES PASS) to 19 modality cells under CHEAP local featurizers. See `README.md` for the gate definition. No em dashes.*

## The gate (unchanged from v1)

A (representation, property) source is admissible content-signal only if the property is readable from CONTENT and survives a leakage-controlled split: **best-cold AUROC >= 0.65 AND shuffled-label selectivity >= 0.10**. `leakage_drop = random - cold` (large = analog-leakage / DTI trap). Cold split = a natural leakage group where one exists (SMILES Murcko scaffold, protein cluster, MSA family, variant gene), else a feature-space KMeans cluster (the modality-general scaffold-split analog). Featurizers are deliberately CHEAP and local (no SFM embedding): the point is to certify signal that survives even a weak encoder.

## Result: 17 / 19 PASS

| task | representation | featurizer | n | base | random | **cold** | drop | **selectivity** | gate |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| admet_bace | SMILES | Morgan r2/2048 | 1513 | 0.46 | 0.892 | **0.871** | +0.021 | +0.409 | **PASS** |
| admet_bbbp | SMILES | Morgan r2/2048 | 2039 | 0.77 | 0.909 | **0.870** | +0.039 | +0.400 | **PASS** |
| admet_hiv | SMILES | Morgan r2/2048 | 4000 | 0.04 | 0.722 | **0.717** | +0.005 | +0.282 | **PASS** |
| withdrawn | SMILES | Morgan r2/2048 | 4000 | 0.28 | 0.745 | **0.668** | +0.077 | +0.232 | **PASS** |
| dna_promoter | DNA seq | 4-mer freq | 3000 | 0.50 | 0.839 | **0.835** | +0.004 | +0.386 | **PASS** |
| rna_coding | RNA seq | 4-mer freq | 1500 | 0.50 | 0.864 | **0.854** | +0.010 | +0.369 | **PASS** |
| protein_meltome | protein seq | AA 2-mer + comp | 1500 | 0.50 | 0.626 | **0.616** | +0.010 | +0.167 | **PASS** |
| msa_conservation | MSA column | AA comp + entropy | 658 | 0.50 | 1.000 | **1.000** | +0.000 | +0.514 | **PASS** |
| variant_clinvar | variant HGVS | char n-gram | 2400 | 0.50 | 0.943 | **0.916** | +0.027 | +0.433 | **PASS** |
| **ppi_name** | **PPI (named)** | char n-gram | 3000 | 0.50 | 0.540 | **0.502** | +0.038 | +0.079 | **fail** |
| **ppi_anon** | **PPI (anon)** | char n-gram | 3000 | 0.50 | 0.513 | **0.497** | +0.016 | -0.002 | **fail** |
| methyl_age | CpG betas | aligned beta vector | 720 | 0.49 | 0.818 | **0.752** | +0.066 | +0.283 | **PASS** |
| ecg5000 | ECG series | raw 140-pt series | 1200 | 0.50 | 0.996 | **0.987** | +0.009 | +0.455 | **PASS** |
| nmr_herg | NMR shifts | shift histogram | 600 | 0.50 | 0.671 | **0.675** | -0.004 | +0.240 | **PASS** |
| single_cell_name | cell sentence (named) | gene bag | 384 | 0.50 | 0.985 | **0.917** | +0.068 | +0.398 | **PASS** |
| single_cell_anon | cell sentence (anon) | token bag | 384 | 0.50 | 0.985 | **0.909** | +0.076 | +0.375 | **PASS** |
| materials_metal | metal formula | element fraction | 4000 | 0.49 | 0.930 | **0.907** | +0.023 | +0.386 | **PASS** |
| glass | glass formula | element fraction | 4000 | 0.71 | 0.758 | **0.664** | +0.094 | +0.247 | **PASS** |
| histo_pcam | H&E patch | pixel color-stat | 400 | 0.50 | 0.766 | **0.698** | +0.068 | +0.338 | **PASS** |

(best-cold = max of logistic and random-forest cold AUROC; the table shows the logistic cold for readability, the gate uses the better of the two.)

## Reads

**1. The verifiable-signal recipe is modality-general (the consolidation result).** 17 of 19 cells carry a content-readable, leakage-controlled signal under a CHEAP encoder, with leakage_drop small everywhere (the signal is not analog leakage collapsing on a cold split) and selectivity high (the probe reads structure, not label noise). v1 showed this for 7 SMILES endpoints; it now holds across sequence (DNA, RNA, protein, MSA), numeric vector (CpG, ECG, NMR), composition (metal, glass), image (H&E), and even a text-rendered variant. The WS2 "mint a verifiable (representation, property) pair, gated by a probe ceiling" method is not SMILES-specific.

**2. PPI fails BOTH named and anon, and that is the real finding (not consolidation).** PPI-as-protein-NAME-text sits at chance under a char n-gram probe (cold 0.502 named, 0.497 anon; selectivity +0.079 / -0.002). This is the SIGNAL-side confirmation of the WS1 anonymization collapse (LLM scored PPI 0.95 named, 0.50 anon). The gate explains WHY: there is NO interaction signal in the surface text of the two protein names, so the LLM's 0.95 was pure web-memorization of documented interactions (a lookup over pretrained pairs), not content-grounding. Anonymize and there is nothing to look up; the named surface form itself never carried the signal. The gate correctly REJECTS PPI-as-name as inadmissible content-signal. (Scope: this rejects the protein-NAME-text representation, not PPI as a task. An ESM/structure embedding featurizer can encode interaction from sequence; the name string cannot. The representation is the lookup key, not the content.)

**3. single-cell PASSES even anonymized, the clean contrast to PPI.** The same anonymization that destroys PPI leaves single-cell almost untouched (0.917 named, 0.909 anon). The reason is structural: a cell's identity lives in the CO-OCCURRENCE pattern of its expressed genes, which is preserved when gene names are swapped for opaque ids (feat_4328), so a token-bag probe still reads cell type. PPI's signal lived only in the web-documented identity of the two names. This is the WS2-side instrument for the WS3 placement axis: **gate-PASS-even-anon = in-data-pattern content (retrieve closes it), gate-FAIL = web-anchor only (no content to retrieve, memory or orchestrate only).** The decision map's "retrieve closes in-data-pattern even anonymized; pure-relational needs orchestrate" is now visible from the signal gate, not just the LLM output arm.

**4. The gate sorts the read-out targets for WS3.** A cell that PASSES the gate AND shows a large verbalization gap in the 3-arm sweep is a real read-out target (the signal exists, the model just cannot say it): methylation (gate 0.752, sweep gap 0.52, ICL closes to 0.93), single-cell, histopath (gate 0.698 even under pixel-stat, VLM ceiling 0.827). A cell that FAILS the gate has no content for a read-out to surface (PPI-name); its only placements are memory (web-rich) or orchestration to a sequence/structure specialist. So the gate is a prospective placement filter, the WS2 to WS3 bridge.

## Named vs anon: the SIGNAL-side web-anchor map (`gate_anon.py`)

For each representation with a named AND an anonymized rendering of the SAME content, the gate on both (best-cold AUROC):

| pair | featurizer | named | anon | Delta | verdict |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| single_cell | gene bag | 0.917 | 0.909 | +0.008 | CONTENT (anon-invariant) |
| methyl_anchor | CpG beta vector | 0.961 | 0.961 | +0.000 | CONTENT (anon-invariant) |
| glass | element fraction | 0.752 | 0.781 | -0.029 | CONTENT (anon-invariant) |
| metal | element fraction | 0.907 | 0.854 | +0.053 | CONTENT (anon-invariant) |
| variant | char n-gram | 0.889 | 0.834 | +0.055 | CONTENT (anon-invariant) |
| **ppi** | char n-gram | **0.594** | **0.497** | +0.097 | **surface-blind (LLM web-memory only)** |

- **5 of 6 are anon-invariant CONTENT.** Stripping the names barely moves the gate (Delta +0.00 to +0.06), because the cheap featurizer reads the DATA PATTERN (gene co-occurrence, CpG beta values, element composition, HGVS syntax), not the identities. This is the SIGNAL-side proof of the placement rule "retrieve closes in-data-pattern even anonymized": the signal that survives anonymization is exactly the signal retrieval can supply.
- **PPI is the only web-anchor.** Named 0.594 (near chance) and anon 0.497: the interaction signal is not in the text surface at all, it lives only in the LLM's pretrained memory of documented pairs. That is why the LLM-output arm scores PPI-named 0.95 while the gate scores 0.59: the 0.36 gap IS the memorization. Anonymize and both arms collapse.
- **The 3-way gate-vs-LLM contrast locates where each signal lives.** gate >> LLM-0shot (methylation gate 0.96 vs LLM 0-shot 0.40) = expression gap (signal in content, LLM cannot verbalize it, read-out / retrieve closes); LLM >> gate (PPI 0.95 vs 0.59) = web-memory (signal in pretraining not content, anonymization collapses it); gate ~ LLM (variant, single-cell) = both read the surface. So comparing the cheap-featurizer gate against the LLM output arm is itself a diagnostic for whether a high LLM number is grounding or memorization.
- **Honest caveat on variant.** anon 0.834 is not pure content: it is residual HGVS SYNTAX (stop-gain / frameshift tokens encode a general pathogenicity rule), the SIGNAL-side version of the Move-3 finding that variant-anon is not fully web-zero. So variant sits between true in-data-pattern (single-cell, methyl) and pure web-anchor (PPI).

## Honest scope

- **Cheap-encoder lower bound.** Featurizers are weak by design (Morgan, k-mer, char n-gram, element fraction, pixel-stat). A PASS is therefore strong evidence (signal survives even a weak encoder). A FAIL means "no signal under THIS encoder", not "no signal": protein_meltome is a borderline PASS (0.616 logistic, RF carries it over 0.65) precisely because AA 2-mer is a poor protein encoder, and an SFM (ESM) would raise it. PPI-name is the genuine reject (a name string has no internal interaction content under any text encoder).
- **Two near-trivial PASSes flagged.** msa_conservation 1.000 is partly definitional (the entropy feature is close to a direct conservation read, so the property is almost the feature); ecg5000 0.987 is a clean shape-classification benchmark. Both are real signal but not surprising.
- **This is consolidation, not a new measurement,** for 16 of 19 cells (they re-confirm the ceilings the 3-arm sweep already measured, now under a cheap shared encoder with the full leakage + selectivity controls). The one piece that behaves like a small new result is the PPI-fail vs single-cell-PASS contrast under anonymization, an independent SIGNAL-side confirmation of the web-anchor-vs-content distinction.

## What this gives the project

- A single modality-general verifiable-signal certificate (the tangible WS2 "signal recipe" artifact for the application, generalizing v1 from 1 modality to 19).
- The WS2 to WS3 bridge made explicit: the gate (content-signal vs web-anchor) predicts the placement (retrieve vs orchestrate/memory) before the LLM output arm is run.
- The verifier-quality reading for the closed-loop / RL story (rBio "cannot exceed its verifier"): the cold AUROC is the per-modality verifier ceiling a read-out or a GRPO reward is bounded by.
